description:
GOAL: Make per-task state real. Introduce `pub struct Process` owning
heap + builders + halt_value. Replace the 5 thread_local!s in
ir_codegen.rs with a single CURRENT_PROCESS TLS slot holding `*mut
Process`. The trampoline installs the current Process before each run
and clears on exit. Unblocks fz-ul4.19.1 by giving it a real per-task
state container; the worker pool + scheduler that drives TLS install /
uninstall at quantum boundaries is .19.1's job.

## Architectural framing (user, 2026-05-11)

We are NOT building "OS-thread-per-process". We are building a
LIBDISPATCH-STYLE concurrency model: many fz processes (tasks) serviced
by a small native worker pool. Pool size for v1 ships at 1
(configurable knob via .19.1's Runtime::new). Abstractions assume task
!= thread from day one; multi-worker is a pool-size change, NOT an
architectural rewrite later.

Implications for this ticket:

- TLS is PER-WORKER, holding "current task ptr". Workers swap tasks at
  cooperative yield boundaries (.19.1). FFI fns read TLS to find their
  task.
- Scheduler invariant ("one worker owns a task while running it")
  provides safety. TLS is the recording mechanism, not the safety
  mechanism.
- FFI fns do not yield mid-call, so TLS is stable within any FFI call.
- No explicit-ctx FFI threading; no Cranelift signature changes; no
  per-call register cost.

## Decisions captured (user, 2026-05-11)

- Q1 — libdispatch model: YES.
- Q2 — SchemaRegistry: Arc<[Schema]> with append-only semantics during
  compile, lock-free reads at runtime. Currently lives inside Heap as
  `Rc<RefCell<SchemaRegistry>>`. This ticket: leave existing structure
  in place (Rc, single-task v1); refactor to Arc<[Schema]> happens
  when .19.1 makes a second worker possible. Note in body for follow-up.
- Q3 — Atom table: single mutex for v1; revisit if intern becomes
  contended. Out of scope for this ticket (see .19.6).
- Q4 — Pool size: configurable via Runtime::new(workers: usize),
  default 1. This is .19.1's responsibility, not .11.32's.

## Why TLS-of-Process-ptr is correct under libdispatch

- Single OS thread (v1 pool size 1): TLS-of-Process-ptr is functionally
  identical to a global Process ptr.
- Multi-worker (future pool size N): each worker has its own TLS slot
  pointing at the task it's currently running. Workers never run two
  tasks at once; tasks never run on two workers at once. TLS update
  happens at task-switch boundary (cooperative yield), not during FFI
  calls.
- Re-entrance footgun: a non-issue. FFI fns don't yield. The task
  switch only happens at known cooperative points (receive, gc
  safepoint, explicit yield builtin).
- Explicit-ctx FFI threading would cost: ~30 signature changes +
  ~50 call site changes + Cranelift signature updates + register
  pressure. With no functional benefit under libdispatch.

## Deliverables

### 1. Process struct (new src/runtime/process.rs or kept in ir_codegen.rs for v1)

```rust
pub struct Process {
    pub heap: Heap,
    pub halt_value: i64,

    // Builders — transient per-call-site state. Per-task (so two
    // tasks can have independent in-flight builders).
    pub map_builder: Option<Vec<(u64, u64)>>,
    pub bs_builder: Option<crate::bitstr::BitWriter>,
    pub vec_builder: Option<VecBuild>,
    pub closure_builder: Option<(u32, Vec<u64>)>,
    pub closure_args: Vec<u64>,

    // Per-CompiledModule constants currently lifted from TLS. .19.1
    // will reconsider: probably move to CompiledModule and have
    // Process hold an Rc to it. For v1 they live here.
    pub frame_sizes: Vec<u32>,
    pub bs_tuple_arity1_schema: Option<u32>,
    pub bs_tuple_arity3_schema: Option<u32>,

    // .19.1 will add: pid, next_frame, mailbox, state.
    // .11.31 may add: gc threshold flag (or fold into Heap).
}

impl Process {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    pub fn with_module(module: &CompiledModule) -> Self;
    // ... constructor variants ...
}
```

### 2. Replace 5 TLS slots with one CURRENT_PROCESS TLS

```rust
thread_local! {
    pub(crate) static CURRENT_PROCESS: std::cell::Cell<*mut Process> =
        std::cell::Cell::new(std::ptr::null_mut());
}

fn current_process<'a>() -> &'a mut Process {
    let p = CURRENT_PROCESS.with(|c| c.get());
    assert!(!p.is_null(), "no Process installed (running outside a worker?)");
    unsafe { &mut *p }
}
```

Every `HEAP.with(|h| h.borrow_mut().alloc_struct(id))` becomes
`current_process().heap.alloc_struct(id)`.

Same pattern for MAP_BUILDER, BS_BUILDER, VEC_BUILDER, CLOSURE_BUILDER,
CLOSURE_ARGS, FRAME_SIZES, BS_TUPLE_ARITY*_SCHEMA.

### 3. Trampoline: install / uninstall CURRENT_PROCESS

```rust
impl CompiledModule {
    pub fn run(&self, fn_id: FnId) -> i64 {
        let mut p = Process::with_module(self);
        self.run_in(fn_id, &mut p)
    }

    pub fn run_in(&self, fn_id: FnId, process: &mut Process) -> i64 {
        let prev = CURRENT_PROCESS.with(|c| c.replace(process as *mut Process));
        // ... existing trampoline body, but using current_process() ...
        CURRENT_PROCESS.with(|c| c.set(prev));
        process.halt_value
    }
}
```

Tests can construct a Process explicitly via `Process::with_module(&compiled)`
and call `run_in`. The convenience `.run(fn_id)` constructs one internally.

### 4. compile() schema registration

Currently `HEAP.with(|h| h.register_schema(s))` registers schemas into
the TLS Heap. Post-migration: compile() needs a SchemaRegistry to
register into. Two options:

A. CompiledModule owns the SchemaRegistry. compile() builds it, embeds
   in CompiledModule. Process::with_module copies the Rc into its Heap.
   This is the v1 path that sets up the Arc<[Schema]>-Q2 follow-up.

B. compile() returns a CompiledModule + initial-schemas tuple; each
   Process is initialized with those schemas. Less clean.

GO WITH (A). compile() registers schemas into a fresh
SchemaRegistry, embeds it in CompiledModule. Process::with_module
constructs a Heap that shares the registry via Rc.

### 5. TLS removal

Delete:
- HEAP (line ~422)
- MAP_BUILDER (line ~487)
- BS_BUILDER + BS_TUPLE_ARITY1_SCHEMA + BS_TUPLE_ARITY3_SCHEMA (line ~609)
- VEC_BUILDER (line ~1232)
- CLOSURE_BUILDER + CLOSURE_ARGS + FRAME_SIZES (line ~1327)

Retain (test-only, comment why):
- TEST_CAPTURE (line ~358)
- IR_TEXT_RECORD (~358)

Add:
- CURRENT_PROCESS (single pub(crate) slot).

### 6. Test helpers

Functions `heap_reset_for_test`, `live_count`, `freelist_len`, `gc`
(test entry) currently use HEAP TLS. They need new shapes:
- Option A: keep as TLS-readers via `current_process()` — works if a
  Process is installed.
- Option B: convert to `&Process` / `&mut Process` methods. Requires
  test scaffolding update.

GO WITH A for least churn; tests install a Process via the trampoline,
helpers read CURRENT_PROCESS. The helpers become thin wrappers.

## Tests (TDD order)

1. Construct a Process via `Process::with_module(&compiled)`; run a
   trivial fn (`fn main(), do: 42`); assert halt_value == 42. Replaces
   existing `compile(...).run()` ergonomic.
2. **TWO-PROCESS TEST (the gating one):** construct two Processes from
   the same CompiledModule, each running a small map-building program
   with disjoint keys via `compiled.run_in(entry, &mut process)`.
   Assert both finish with correct distinct map values. PRE-MIGRATION
   this fails because MAP_BUILDER is shared TLS.
3. Sequential interleaving: run proc A for one fn, then proc B, then
   back to A. Confirms CURRENT_PROCESS install/uninstall is correct.
4. SAMPLE-based tests still pass (regression).
5. No regression in any existing JIT test.

## Scope NOT in this ticket

- Worker pool / Scheduler / quantum mechanics — .19.1.
- Multi-worker concurrency — pool size knob in .19.1; this ticket
  remains single-task at any moment.
- Schema audit for tracing GC root walker — .11.31.
- GC safepoints in trampoline — .11.31.
- Explicit-ctx FFI threading — NOT FILED, NOT NEEDED.
- Atom table sync — .19.6.

## Risks

- Mechanical churn: ~50 call sites change shape (`HEAP.with(...)` →
  `current_process().heap...`). Mitigation: mechanical search-and-
  replace; the helper makes each site one line.
- Test helper migration may surface oddities. Mitigation: TDD order
  above; SAMPLE regression test catches anything subtle.
- Drop semantics on Process: heap holds raw allocations; Drop must
  release. Verify; the existing Heap presumably handles its own Drop.

## References

- src/ir_codegen.rs:124-154 (trampoline; gains install/uninstall)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:158-160 (HostCtx — REMOVED; replaced by Process)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:422 (HEAP TLS — DELETE)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:487 (MAP_BUILDER TLS — DELETE)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:609 (BS_BUILDER + schemas TLS — DELETE)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:1232 (VEC_BUILDER TLS — DELETE)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:1327 (CLOSURE_BUILDER etc TLS — DELETE)
- src/heap.rs:73-96 (Heap struct, owns Rc<RefCell<SchemaRegistry>>)
- fz-ul4.19.1 — worker pool + scheduler that drives this Process

## Acceptance

- Process struct lives on its own (transitionally inside ir_codegen.rs
  or in a new module).
- CURRENT_PROCESS replaces the 5 TLS slots.
- HostCtx is gone.
- compiled.run() and compiled.run_in(entry, &mut Process) both work.
- Two-Process map test passes.
- Full test suite green; warnings-as-errors clean.
- One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.32.
title: Per-Process runtime context: thread ctx ptr through FFI (de-TLS)
